exercise_name: Alternating Full Step Up to Knee Raise
coaching_cues: Drive through the top leg and control the descent.

videos.description:
Alternating Full Step Up to Knee Raise

I love that this movement takes out the confusion of which leg stays on the bench on the way down. We want to make sure members drive through the top leg and control the descent. As they get into rhythm, it is easy to start pushing off the bottom foot. It is very hard to see visually, so we just want cue “are you driving through the top leg”? “Push the top foot through the bench, stretching the leg long”. Another great one I heard (I believe from @will.liptak for a step down but regardless still works) is only place your heel on the ground, even just the cue of lifting your toes will help take drive off the bottom foot.

#alternatingfullstepuptokneeraise #stepups #stepuptokneedrive #orangetheory #otfcoach

exercise_name: Bear Plank Hold Same core muscles as the high plank, but a shorter lever

videos.description: Bear Plank Hold Same core muscles as the high plank, but a shorter lever. This shorter lever gives us the chance to really focus on the upper body and working protraction. Universally with planks members can choose how much energy they exert. There is low threshold strategies where members are using as little muscle force then they absolute need. Just the opposite would be a high threshold strategy where members feel like they are going to pass out if they hold that force for more than a minute. They can make it as easy or tough as they want.  #bearplank #bearplankhold #otf #coach #orangetheory

coaching_cues: Stay as parallel to the ground as possible to engage the lats.
Pull the elbows back behind the hips to reduce trap dominance.

videos.description:
Bench Seated Low Row

The more parallel we can be to the ground, the easier it will be to truly engage the lats. Many members will find it hard to breathe or maybe not even get close to that position due to tightness in the hamstrings or lower back. Most will find more of a 45° angle. 
Coach Tony @konidaris1207 gives a great cue of moving the dumbbells back like an escalator. Many also respond well to cueing pulling the elbows back behind the hips because regardless of torso angle they will reduce trap dominance and engage lats. Try and use them both. Share in the comments if you have any other cues that work well. 

#seatedlowrow #otfbenchseatedlowrow #otfseatedlowrow #orangetheory #otfcoach

Reciprocating Ipsilateral Bicycle Press This is one we have to be super clear in the demo so members aren’t staring at the screen trying to figure out which leg is bent/extended. “So is it same said straight or opposite side straight?”  It is also helpful to start off with both the same leg and arm on one side straight and the other side bent, then starting the reciprocating process.  Once these two major concepts are understood, we can focus on a neutral spine, particularly lumbar and cervical.  #otf #otfcoach #reciprocatingipsilateralbicyclepress #core #orangetheory

videos.description:
Dip in Push Press

It is NOT a squat to shoulder press (meaning a superficial squat, then pause). The whole point of involving the hips is either more shoulder explosive power or to work from core to smaller extremities ie our strong hips/legs into our shoulders aka a transfer of power. 

If we do this with weight that we can normally shoulder press, goal would be to move it faster. Ideal we would be using weight that is tough to shoulder press so we NEED the momentum and drive from our hips. 

The depth of the dip is only as shallow as we need. The heavier the weight or the more the fatigue, the deeper the leg drive. The dip is a very vertical movement to stack the weight correctly and channel momentum vertical.

#dipinapushpress #otfpushpress #pushpressdip #orangetheory #otfcoach

Floor Single Arm Close Grip Chest Press

Floor presses are generally used to build lock out strength particularly at the triceps. Shorter range of motion should allow us to use more weight. To get the most out of it, we want to place the elbow down and pause to remove the strength reflex. 
Working a single arm, allows us to use both arms to get the weight set up and situated. Teach members this process early on, so even if they don’t need it now, they know and understand it if they want to challenge themselves to something obnoxiously heavy but safely.

Skier swing: Members tend to understand hip power in the skier swing more than a hip hinge swing. Here are two faults to keep an eye out for! #otf #orangetheory #skierswings #coach #faults

Hollow Hold

Stemming from a gymnastics hollow body hold, this isometric movement is all about the core. Squeezing the abs to form a C shaped figure. Biggest faults we see is arching in the lower back and more so rounding of the neck/c-spine. Have members concentrate on closing the space between the lower back and the floor as well as leaving the neck open where we can fit an orange under the chin. 

#hollowhold #otfhollowhold #hollowbodyhold #gymnastics #otfcoach

coaching_cues: The triceps extension we want to only move from the elbow up

videos.description:
Hollow Hold Triceps Extension

The hollow hold stems from a gymnastic shape called the hollow body position. We are seeking a core challenged position with a neutral or slight posterior tilted pelvis/lower back. The triceps extension we want to only move from the elbow up. We can cue elbow highest to the ceiling, elbow stay stacked over the arm pits, the elbow is a hinge… members often go too heavy and limit total range of motion.

exercise_name: Ipsilateral Single Arm Split Stance Deadlift
coaching_cues: Keep the knee over the ankle with a mostly vertical shin; keep an eye out for a straight leg with a negative shin angle
Keep the hips square and the dumbbell close

videos.description:
Ipsilateral Single Arm Split Stance Deadlift

In any b-stance/split stance deadlift we want to keep majority of weight in the front leg so it helps to take a shallow step back with the toe planted. If members step back too far, most end up putting too much weight in the back leg. Keep the knee over the ankle with a mostly vertical shin; keep an eye out for a straight leg with a negative shin angle. Keep the hips square and the dumbbell close.

Remind members, this is ipsilateral. Usually I cue dumbbell fills the empty space but this one “which ever hand has the dumbbell, the same leg is forward”.

#ipsilateralsinglearmsplitstancedeadlift #ipsilateralbstancedeadlift #bstance #deadlifts #otfcoach

coaching_cues: We want to really watch this position from the front view

videos.description: Bent Arm Lateral Raise We see lateral raises often so this bent arm position provides some variance. It also reduces the stress on the wrist and less variability with the position of the elbow.  We want to really watch this position from the front view. To maximize the effectiveness, we want the hands directly in front of the elbows. Often members are going to bring the hands inward. This reduces the overall load brining the weight in from the end of the lever. While we can always use it as an option, to get the most out of this hands would be in directly in front of the elbows.  #bentarmlateralraise #lateralraise #shoulders #delts #deltoids

exercise_name: Low Bench Alternating Step Down Reverse Lunge to Hammer Curl
coaching_cues: Make sure members are getting deeper into the lunge
Weight choice will be limited by the strength of the hammer curl so should be on the lighter side for a deeper lunge

videos.description:
Low Bench Alternating Step Down Reverse Lunge to Hammer Curl

The low bench is used to create more range of motion. Make sure members are getting deeper into the lunge. If they are just doing a superficial step back, they are better off doing a flat ground lunge. Weight choice will be limited by the strength of the hammer curl so should be on the lighter side for a deeper lunge. 

#lowbenchalternatingstepdownreverselungetohammercurl #lowbench #deficitreverselunge #hammercurl #otfcoach

Low Bench Box Jump

This is going to be more comfortable for some members than a flat ground jump squat with the reduction of landing impact. It’s not necessarily wrong, but it is safest to have them land full foot on the bench and step down. 
It will be intimidating for some, so have some prep to build them up as well as some options to keep them working on power output but being safe and successful! 

#otflowbenchboxjump #boxjump #plyometrics #orangetheory #otfcoach

Quite often members don’t have the mobility to straighten the leg in the single leg squat aka the pistol position. They are also often surprise on their ability to achieve success with this option.

Make sure we have options to have our members safe but also progressing to control in a bigger range of motion while staying explosive.

videos.description: Bear Plank to Alternating Knee Tap to Push Up Make sure members start off with a wide knee/foot base that allows a more stable position to keep the hold steady when we go through the knee tap.  When it comes to the push up, members often crane their necks, shorting the range of motion. It will help to give them a higher point of focus to keep a neutral neck.  The kneel mat can help to make sure members return to a good bear position. We can also encourage a knee push up if needed for a better range of motion.  #otf #coach #otfcoach #bearplanktoalternatingkneetaptopushup #bearplankkneetappushup

Burpee (No Push Up aka Up Down) Encourage members to try to keep the heels planted when the hands find the ground to relieve pressure in the ankle/knee. Important that we try to maintain a flat back in this explosive movement. Generally can go feet inside hands or feet out but always helpful to land wide on the return.  #burpee #burpeenopushup #updown #otf #coach

Alternating Burpee to Bench Lateral Step Over No push up on the burpee; one burpee one full step over. So if we have the space, burpees are done on both sides of the bench. If you are even questioning safety or you think it will be close, have members only do burpees on one side of the bench (but have them turn opposite direction half way). #alternatingburpeetobenchlateralstepover #burpeestepover #burpee #otf #otfcoach

Side to Side Push Up Very similar to our step out motions we are really working a weight transfer from hand to hand. Normal push up ideal form, neutral spine (probably eye focus 1-2’ forward), chest touching/thighs do not, corkscrew the hands into the ground with a slight tuck of the elbows.  If we use knees as our option, make sure members use the kneel mat to reduce friction/burn on the knees. If members can’t do them from toes but knees are too easy, we can elevate them up to a bench.  #pushups #sidetosidepushups #otf #otfcoach #alternatingaidepushups

coaching_cues: Make sure knee tracks 2nd/3rd toe
You can always step around a less agressive angle to control knee tracking
Make sure to step back and out
Don’t plant the foot then squat
If the knee collapses inside of the big toe, we get excessive stress on the lateral stabilizers of the base leg knee

videos.description: Refining the transverse squat! We do a lot of saggital plane movements and even some frontal plane but not too much in the transverse plane.  1. Don’t plant the foot then squat. If you plant the foot then squat. The tranverse plane only worked to get the foot into place, then you are doing a regular squat. Think rotate and squat simultaneously.  2. If the knee collapses inside of the big toe, we get excessive stress on the lateral stabilizers of the base leg knee. Make sure knee tracks 2nd/3rd toe. You can always step around a less agressive angle to control knee tracking.  3. If the base foot moves, we are taking away from the tranverse plane and getting closer to a transverse lunge vs a squat. Glue that foot in place.  4. Stepping back in-line with the base leg puts excessive rotation at either the knee or hip. Make sure to step back and out. You can always use a bench as a marker to ensure you are stepping out and around.  Enjoy and keep those knees safe!  #otf #orangetheory #coach #tranversesquat #transverseplane #squat

videos.description: Lateral Bound to Reverse Lunge Plyometrics are generally categorized into either short response or long response. Lateral bounds are a long response where we take time to load, explode, and then absorb force.  To keep members successful start off with a small distance to get used of the balance it requires in the lateral movement. As they move through the timed section, ideally they would open up more range still controlling the balance.  With this as a primer, we want to move through a big range of motion in the lunge, opening up range at the hip and knee.  #otf #coach #otfcoach #lateralboundtoreverselunge #lateralbounds

Alternating Reverse Lunge Reverse lunges are often easier on the knees than forward lunges. We do want to keep an upright torso vs hinging. We should also be pretty analytical when it comes to options. Can we maintain the intended stimulus (heavy, endurance, TUT…)? Can we maintain the intended movement pattern target (squat, hinge, lunge…)? Can we maintain unilateral vs bilateral? All of this should be considered for the given option.  #alternatjngreverselunge #reverselunge #lunge #otf #otfcoach

Alternating Forward Lunge Even though the majority of lunge we see are reverse lunges. There is a good amount of value in a forward lunge with an upright torso. Not only is a bit more quad biased, it gives us an opportunity to strengthen the musculature around the knee. If there is discomfort at the knee, we can always have the try stepping out further, or even going to that reverse lunge.  #alternatingforwardlunge #lunges #quads #otf #otfcoach

Alternating Neutral Grip Push Press The whole intention of the dip is to gain a little power but the shoulder is still working a good amount. The depth of the dip is dependent on how much help the shoulder needs. If the weight is easy to moderate, the dip is shallow. The more fatigue that sets into the shoulder, the deeper the dip should get. Members should choose a weight too heavy to do multiple reps of a shoulder press. A good option would just to do an alternating explosive shoulder press.  #alternatingneutralgrippushpress #dumbbellalternatingpushpress #pushpress #shoulders #otf

Biceps Curl to Shoulder Press Solely in the curl we really focus on the importance of elbow position as it changes the leverage/momentum arm. Since this is a combination movement, it’s ok if the elbow moves forward of the torso since we are transitioning into a shoulder press. I follow the VVA but it is helpful to do both palms facing forward and palms rotating inwards when it comes to curls. For the shoulder press, idea palms face forward with elbow to the side. For some elbow in front of torso will help, for other palms fully facing in on a neutral grip. Last, if member are tight through the shoulders or are having a hard time bracing the core, we can always offer a staggered stance to help. #bicepscurltoshoulderpress #curltopress #combinationmovement #otf #coach

Single Leg Shoulder Press This movement is going to be a ton more challenging for members than it seems, especially with cushioned running shoes with a high stack height. Might have to remind members: balance only gets better if we work on it. Focal points matter! If they use the toe down let’s be strategic on placement and remind them to only use it as much as they need. If we use lateral raises or scaption, just keep in mind center of mass is changing and balance may be more difficult.  #singlelegshoulderpress #balanceshoulderpress #balance #otf #otfcoach

Seated Reciprocating Shoulder Press Reciprocating is going to require coordination and stabilization. The seated posting is going to restrict any contribution from the legs as we fatigue. As it reciprocates, it may create some space in the AC joint for some, if there is still pinching we can try neutral grip or his go to a lateral raise. It’s important for us as coaches to demo heavy so they know it should be a struggle. It doesn’t have to be your true weight but it should be heavy. It is also important that we know how to explain how to get setup for heavy safely and teach them even if they don’t need it yet. #seatedreciprocatingshoulderpress #shoulderpress #seatedshoulderpress #orangetheory #otfcoach

exercise_name: Single Arm Clean Technically should be titled a hang power clean
coaching_cues: Single Arm Clean Technically should be titled a hang power clean

videos.description: Single Arm Clean Technically should be titled a hang power clean. (Hang: starting position, power: noting it is received in a high squat vs a full squat) this movement is all about a big violent hip extension followed by a rapid small retract, so an opening and closing of the hip.  Best thing we can do is to simplifying for the members; jump the weight up to shoulder height, and land in a baby squat. Just jump and land. This has nothing to do with a curl and I dislike it as an option personally. If member need an option find something to mirror or mimic and aggressive hip opening (ideally followed by a quick close, but at least the hip extension) #hangpowerclean #singlearmhangpowerclean #singlearmclean #dumbbellclean #power

Single Arm High Row

We are using the high row to target the lower trap/rhomboids. When we cue the elbow position, some times it’s challenging for members since they don’t have a good visual and can’t see the elbow. A helpful cue that I’ve been using is have them think about bringing their hand as far out to the side of their body. videos.description: Single arm snatch: Great opportunity tomorrow to take advantage of the rehearse option we have. Only two floor exercises to demo so plenty of time to teach a progression for the single arm snatch.  #otf #orangetheory #coach #snatch #progressions #rehearse
Keeping a neutral spine with the core locked in when pulling an alternating single arm snatch from the ground.  #otf #orangetheory #coach #fitness #snatch

Alternating Single Arm Snatch There are a lot of technical pieces to this movement, but the best thing we can do as coaches is to simplify it; jump and punch. We want to be clear that this is a hip power movement, not an arm movement. Jump the weight up and punch at the top. Arms do little to no work. We want the dumbbell to move in as straight of a line possible.  Most important is to drop the hips low before jumping the weight up!  #alternatingsinglearmsnatch #dumbellsnatch #singlesarmsnatch #otf #otfcoach

coaching_cues: Intention is single leg force and lateral control and balance

videos.description: Speed skater lunges and speed skaters with power. Intention is single leg force and lateral control and balance. As coaches, when we give options individually we have to dog deeper on why are we prescribing the option. What can we do better to help them advance instead of generic options like less weight, limited range of motion 🙄 (those are obvious always). Let’s always think about what the intention of the exercise is and how can we give them small steps to be better everyday. #otf #orangetheory #fitness #coach #coaching #speedskater
Speed skater lunges and speed skaters with power.

coaching_cues: Keep hips and toes pointed forward while moving laterally with power and control.
Reach toward the ground to load the hip; use a lateral lunge with overhead reach to reduce impact.

videos.description:
Speed Skater with Power

A lot of similarities with a speed skater. Hips/toes stay pointed forward, lateral power and control. With power we are adding a little more force, which most likely show with a higher height. We are also reaching, if not touching the ground to load the hip and be more explosive. A great option would be a lateral lunge with reach to overhead to reduce impact. 

#speedskaterwithpower #speedskater #lateralpower #orangetheory #otfcoach

coaching_cues: Especially as we work a slow descent we might need to slightly tip the torso forward for better weight distribution
This option gives them the chance to focus weight in the front leg and a big range of motion while providing them a little more balance and control

videos.description: Front Loaded Contralateral Single Arm Split Squat (Slow) In all split squats we want the feet to be about shoulder width apart. We want majority of weight in the front leg, maybe 70-80% of weight. Especially as we work a slow descent we might need to slightly tip the torso forward for better weight distribution. When it comes to that deliberate slow count, often members push too much weight in the back leg. We need constant reminders to be heavy in the front leg.  It is much more challenging at tempo with up high and away from the hips, if members are having a hard time shifting weight forward, we can always have them use the option of weight down by their side. If they have balance issues, we can always move to ipsilateral loading as well.  #otf #coach #frontloadedcontralateralsinglearmsplitsquatslow #frontloadedcontralateralsinglearmsplitsquat #balance

Goblet Rear Foot Elevated Split Squat Option (Bulgarian Split Squat Option) Often members are hesitant on this movement due to mobility or balance issues. This option gives them the chance to focus weight in the front leg and a big range of motion while providing them a little more balance and control.  #gobletrearfootelevatedsplitsquat #rfess #bulgariansplitsquat #benchgobletrearfootelevatedsplitsquat #balance

Seated Biceps Curl to Stand In isolated biceps work, we are very focused on staying disciplined on the elbow position. With the elbow at or slightly behind the ribs, it keeps tension on the biceps. We allow the elbow to move forward as we transition into this movement as we combine the seated curl with a stand. #seatedbicepscurltostand #curltoboxsquat #curltosquat #otf #otfcoach

Single Arm Step Out Squat Moving an outside asymmetrical load to the center for a symmetrical squa and then back out. A lighter, smaller dumbbell is easy to maneuver. When it comes to a larger heavier dumbbell, it helps to not move it all the way to the outside of the hip. Palm facing will be enough to still move the weight to an asymmetrical position in the stat but reduce the compensations of torso to accommodate a large heavy dumbbell.  #singlearmstepoutsquat #stepoutsquat #otf #otfcoach #squa

exercise_name: Squat Jump with Elbows Wide It’s always important that we have a “why”

videos.description: Squat Jump with Elbows Wide It’s always important that we have a “why”? The elbows wide, aka prisoner squat position, lifts the chest and keeps the back upright and slightly extended. We want members to shoot for height in their jumps and be as explosive as possible. Important that we coach a quiet landing as well, teaching them how to muscularly absorb force. If they are not jumpers, we still want them as explosive as possible but keeping their toes on the ground.  #otf #coach #otfcoach #squatjumpwithelbowswide #prisonersquatjump

Squat to Alternating Contralateral Single Arm Upward Rotation

Squat to Shrug

Most of our members aren’t looking to build bigger thicker traps. We need to emphasize the mobility of our scaps promotes better overall shoulder health. So this is more about movement and control, than it is about muscle growth. If they are worried about it they can add some slight retraction and they will feel it more in the mid to low trap but the upper traps will still work as they have to elevate the shoulders.

#squattoshrug #otfsquattoshrug #scapularelevation #shoulderhealth #otfcoach

exercise_name: The dumbbell clean (technically a hang power clean)

videos.description: The dumbbell clean (technically a hang power clean). Intention is to express power at the hip on an external load. Use full extention to maximize power output, then retract the hips to sink under the load at a point of it’s apex. Then using the legs to stand the load upright. While there is a shrug and an aggressive extension at the ankles, we generally don’t cue that much. When athletes think about those features it tends to slow the movement and make it choppy.  KISS principle. Stuck to simple cuing; “Jump the weight up and land under,” “jump under the weights,” “tall before you get small”… Be as creative as you want and find something that jives with your speech. #otf #orangetheory #cleans #power #weightlifting

videos.description:
Ultimate Burpee with Neutral Grip Thruster

Flat back, down with a wide stance push up,
Row both arms, stand with a flat back jump the weights into a squat and thruster. Have members think of simplistic patterns to understand the flow. Slow is smooth and smooth is fast. 
The option to elevate is going to be helpful for both members that going to the ground is challenging and/or if the have any wrist compression issues.

What does a wood chopper work?

Why floor presses? Powerlifters use it to improve weak sticking points usually the lockout. It reduces range of motion so can go heavier and easier on the shoulder joint. Also, the pause takes away the stretch reflex.

coaching_cues: We want to reach the arms long over head
Make sure the bell is moving directly over the head

videos.description: Y-Bell Standing Rainbow We often see this movement at the rowers. We want to reach the arms long over head. Make sure the bell is moving directly over the head. It is sometimes shifted forward due to loss of core tension and/or tight shoulders. Last, stabilize the core and ensure the body is not shifting side to side as the Y Bell moves up and over.  #ybellstandingrainbow #standingrainbow #ybell #otf #otfcoach

coaching_cues: Let’s have them actively drive the hands away from the shoulders
Core and bodyline rigid, no bends at the elbows and control the eccentric/lowering phase

videos.description: Y-Raise: Most OTF exercises ask for shoulder blades to be back and down (retracted and depressed). This is one of the few movements that actually call for scapular elevation and upward rotation. If we get members into a great starting position, it doesn’t take an aggressive angle to challenge the movement. Let’s have them actively drive the hands away from the shoulders. Always slight tension on the straps, no saggy straps. Core and bodyline rigid, no bends at the elbows and control the eccentric/lowering phase.  #otf #orangetheory #coach #yraises #trx #fitness
